{{Quote|Just because it's a suicide mission doesn't mean we have to step into the noose willingly.|Hanc Thorben|<ref name="arbra">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030605arbra|text=Arbra: Sanctuary In the Storm|int=Arbra: Sanctuary In the Storm}}</ref>}}
'''Hanc Thorben''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] officer who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] holding the rank of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]]. Stationed at [[Haven Base]] on the planet [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]], he also served as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] for the flight group known as [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Thorben was a member of the four-person [[Flying Bantha Squadron]] during the Rebels' efforts to destroy the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[teezl]] device in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], personally overseeing the refitting of four Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE starfighters]] before flying in the [[Operation Earplug|mission]] himsel.

==Biography==
{{Quote|My team of tech specialists has made several modifications that will ensure success, though I still have doubts that this will be anything but a one-way trip. However, if that's the way it has to be, that's the way it has to be.|Hanc Thorben|<ref name="arbra" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Hanc Thorben put his [[Technician/Legends|technical]] skills to use in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], reaching the rank of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="marvel60">[[Star Wars (1977) 60|''Star Wars'' (1977) 60]]</ref> A member of the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]],<ref name="negtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Thorben was tapped to be one of four Rogue [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] forming a special unit known as [[Flying Bantha Squadron]] for a [[Operation Earplug|mission]] of dire importance at the height of the war. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had discovered a potent natural [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] communicator called the [[teezl]] on the fringes of known [[Realspace/Legends|space]], one which could facilitate instantaneous mass coordination between all aspects of the Imperial war machine. Seeing this as a potential game-changer, Thorben and the Flying Banthas—under the command of [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Yavin]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—were tasked with making sure the teezl never reached the [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial capital]].<ref name="marvel65">[[Star Wars (1977) 65|''Star Wars'' (1977) 65]]</ref>

[[File:Thorben2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Captain Hanc Thorben at Spindrift.]]
The Flying Banthas elected to approach this task through subterfuge, acquiring four reconstructed Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE starfighters]] that would allow them to blend in with the [[Secret armada|massive armada]] convoying the teezl.<ref name="marvel65" /> Thorben directly oversaw the preparation of these craft as Refit Supervisor, heading a team of technical specialists to make several key adjustments, including bolstered [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] power, the addition of extra [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] and an ejector seat—although Thorben believed it to be a suicide mission, he wanted to still give his team their best shot at returning alive.<ref name="arbra" /> Thorben and the Flying Banthas tested these successfully in a [[Skirmish on Spindrift|sortie]] at the Imperial outpost of [[Spindrift]]—afterward, Thorben searched the Imperial base and discovered the route coordinates of the Imperial armada, giving them every tool they would need to pull off the raid. Soon after the raid on Spindrift, Thorben joined Skywalker and other Rogues in a mission to [[Shalyvane]], the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of fellow Flying Bantha [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]], in order to provide Brie support as she performed a vital religious rite to her people. The Rebels were ambushed by [[Em'liy/Legends|Em'liy]] natives and pinned down in a vicious firefight, only escaping when Brie slipped around the Em'liy force and strafed their attackers with her [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]].<ref name="marvel60" />

When the time came to launch the raid on the teezl's armada, Thorben joined Skywalker, Brie and [[Alph (Rogue)|Alph]] in obtaining recognition codes for the Imperial convoy from a [[Milnn|captured TIE pilot]] before infiltrating the armada itself. Once in formation, the Flying Banthas struck out from within, causing chaos among confused Imperial forces that couldn't immediately identify where the enemy was. Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mils Giel]] eventually countered by ordering the teezl to jam Rebel transmissions—cut off from the rest of his squadron, Thorben sustained damage severe enough that he had to withdraw from the battle, returning to [[Haven Base]] on [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]]. Thorben wound up being one of only two Flying Banthas to make it home safely, as Skywalker managed to destroy the teezl and return victorious—however, Thorben greeted Skywalker with ill news, as the flight commander was immediately placed under investigation for apparently shooting down Brie in an act of friendly fire.<ref name="marvel61">[[Star Wars (1977) 61|''Star Wars'' (1977) 61]]</ref>

To Thorben's relief, Skywalker was ultimately cleared of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] charges when it was uncovered that Brie was an Imperial double agent. In the immediate aftermath of the not-guilty verdict, Thorben was conversing with fellow Rebel officer [[Gemmer Sojan/Legends|Gemmer Sojan]] when a remark Sojan made about a glowing paperweight from the former Rebel base at [[Golrath Station]] sparked his curiosity. After examining the trinket, Thorben realized that [[Golrath/Legends|Golrath]] stone had a unique ability to replay events that had occurred in its vicinity, potentially meaning Rebel secrets could be exposed to Imperials investigating the facility. Thorben immedaitely reported this news to Rebel leadership, and a subsequent [[Raid on Golrath Station|raid on Golrath]] ended in the station's destruction.<ref name="marvel65">[[Star Wars (1977) 65|''Star Wars'' (1977) 65]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|That's quite a story, lass! Too bad it don't do beans about gettin' us out of this scrape!|Hanc Thorben, to Shira Brie|<ref name="marvel60" />}}
A balding, taciturn man,<ref name="marvel60" /> Hanc Thorben's technical expertise was integral to the success of Flying Bantha Squadron's attack on the teezl—although personally pessimistic that he would survive, Thorben diligently worked to give himself and his fellow pilots the best possible chance to return home safely.<ref name="arbra" /> Thorben had a strong respect for his flight commander, Luke Skywalker, believing him to be exactly the sort of man the Rebellion needed to win the war.<ref name="marvel65" /> Although highly regarded for his technical skills, Thorben was also a talented starfighter pilot and capable in a close-range [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fight.<ref name="marvel60" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hanc Thorben appeared in several issues of the original [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Comics ''Star Wars'' run]], where he was written by [[David Michelinie]] and [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]] and illustrated by Simonson<ref name="marvel60" /><ref name="marvel61" /> and [[Tom Palmer]].<ref name="marvel65" /> The [[2003]] [[Cory J. Herndon]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] article ''[[Arbra: Sanctuary In the Storm]]'' contained a section written from Thorben's point-of-view, and was the first source to place his first name as "Hanc."<ref name="arbra" />
